title: Determine the exact value of R(3,10)
micro_paper_assessment: Still attractive if closed, but the current solve output is only a structural reduction and is too thin on its own for the micro-paper lane.
family_affinity: Strong off-diagonal triangle-versus-clique Ramsey anchor; the 40-vertex boundary reduces directly to interaction with near-extremal R(3,9) residual graphs.
generalization_signal: Moderate; the closed-neighborhood complement and independent-hitting-set reduction should transfer to nearby one-step R(3,k) gaps, but actual closure remains instance-specific.
candidate_theorem_slice: Any triangle-free graph on 40 vertices with independence number at most 9 must satisfy 4 <= delta <= Delta <= 9 and chi >= 5, and each closed-neighborhood complement lies in a narrow (3,9)-near-extremal window with a sharply bounded anticomplete set to the neighborhood.
next_action: Import concrete 39-vertex witness or upper-bound residual-family data and test the one-vertex extension obstruction, especially in the degree-4 and degree-5 cases.
